violations.text: Sushi bar ingredients were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit at onset of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of staff, sushi bar refrigerator is turned on daily. Sushi bar refrigerator must be chilled to 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below prior to placing food. Foods were placed into walk-in freezer to blast chill. Sushi bar refrigerator chilled to 35 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025

Time as a Public Health Control is used for sushi rice. Logs are not being maintained; logs were photocopied with prefilled information. Permission to use Time as a Public Health Control is contingent upon active managerial control and maintenance of records. Logs must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
Screen door in back area is not fitting flush (back door OK). New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2025

The cutting boards in the are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: -shelf between 3 bay sink and dishmachine -partition at back prep room handsink New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the hibachi area countertop frames have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other appropriate method Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

(Garlic in oil) was observed held at (68) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of staff, garlic and oil was removed from refrigeration at opening. Garlic in oil was placed in freezer to regain temperature control during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2024
Food was observed on the floor in the walk-freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other appropriate method Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
Restrooms did not have handwashing signs. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Garlic in oil was observed held at (67) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of staff, garlic and oil was removed from refrigeration at opening. Garlic in oil was placed into refrigeration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

Establishment is using Time as a Public Health Control to hold sushi rice. Records are not being maintained. Sushi rice logs must be maintained daily per batch and kept on file for inspector review for 90 days. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
Food was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets for 2 door lowboy were observed in poor repair in kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2024

Wooden container is used for sushi rice. Food and non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

Fried frozen calamari were observed stored in an unlined cardboard box. PIC informed to cease practice. Food was placed into other food grade containers during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the walk-in cooler and the fans guard behind prep unit have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024

The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other appropriate method. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2024
Pooling water was observed in the women's restroom. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2024
